questions.q: What kind of army was the Vanara Sena?
options: An army of monkeys and bears
questions.explanation: The Vanara Sena was the great host of vanaras (monkey-folk) and bears who rallied to Rama's cause.

questions.q: Whom did the Vanara Sena serve and fight for?
questions.explanation: The vanara army rallied to Rama to help him recover Sita from Ravana.

questions.q: Which great structure did the Vanara Sena build to reach Lanka?
questions.explanation: The vanaras built the great bridge (Setu) across the ocean so Rama's army could cross to Lanka.

questions.q: Which island kingdom did the Vanara Sena storm?
questions.explanation: The vanara army crossed the ocean and stormed Lanka, the kingdom of Ravana.

questions.q: Who was the vanara king whose army joined Rama?
questions.explanation: Sugriva, king of the vanaras of Kishkindha, allied with Rama and committed his army to the cause.

questions.q: Which mighty vanara of the army leapt across the ocean to find Sita in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the greatest of the vanara warriors, leapt across the ocean to Lanka and found Sita.

questions.q: From which kingdom did most of the Vanara Sena come?
questions.explanation: The vanara army was drawn chiefly from Kishkindha, the mountain realm of the monkey-folk ruled by Sugriva.

questions.q: Besides monkeys, what other kind of creatures fought in the army for Rama?
questions.explanation: Bears (riksha) fought alongside the vanaras, most famously under their leader Jambavan.

questions.q: Why did the Vanara Sena go to war against Ravana?
questions.explanation: The army marched to help Rama rescue Sita, whom Ravana had abducted to Lanka.

questions.q: What did the vanaras primarily use as weapons in battle?
questions.explanation: The vanaras fought mainly by hurling boulders, trees, and mountain peaks, and by striking with their bare strength.

questions.q: Which vanara, son of Vali, served as the heir-apparent and a leading commander of the army?
questions.explanation: Angada, the son of Vali, was the crown prince of Kishkindha and a chief commander among the vanara host.

questions.q: Which two vanara brothers, sons of celestial architects, supervised the building of the bridge to Lanka?
questions.explanation: The vanara engineers Nala and Nila led the construction of the great bridge across the sea.

questions.q: Who was the wise old bear-king who led the bears within the army and counseled the vanaras?
questions.explanation: Jambavan, the venerable king of the bears, was a wise elder and commander allied with Rama's army.

questions.q: Sugriva sent search parties of the army in all four directions. Which direction led to the discovery of Sita?
questions.explanation: The southern search party, which included Hanuman and Angada, ultimately learned where Sita was held and reached Lanka.

questions.q: Which aged vulture, brother of Jatayu, told the southern search party where Sita had been taken?
questions.explanation: Sampati, the wingless brother of Jatayu, told the vanaras that Ravana had carried Sita across the sea to Lanka.

questions.q: What was the name commonly given to the great bridge the vanaras built to Lanka?
questions.explanation: The bridge is traditionally called Rama Setu or Nala Setu, after Rama and the vanara engineer Nala who built it.

questions.q: Which vanara warrior was the commander-in-chief (senapati) of Sugriva's army in the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Nila served as the senapati, the commander of the vanara forces, marshalling the host in battle.

questions.q: Which vanara physician of the army revived Lakshmana and others using the Sanjivani herb?
questions.explanation: Sushena, the vanara healer, prescribed the Sanjivani herb to revive the fallen, which Hanuman fetched on the mountain.

questions.q: When the southern search party reached the ocean and despaired, who reminded Hanuman of his power to leap across?
questions.explanation: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his immense, forgotten powers, rousing him to leap the ocean to Lanka.

questions.q: Where did the vanara army assemble before crossing the sea to Lanka?
options: The southern seashore near the ocean's edge
questions.explanation: The army gathered on the southern seashore, from where the bridge to Lanka was begun.

questions.q: How were the vanaras and bears generally said to be born, giving the army its supernatural strength?
options: As offspring of gods and celestial beings
From the foam of the sea
questions.explanation: The vanaras and bears were said to be sired by gods and celestial beings to aid Rama, giving them great strength.

questions.q: Which rakshasa brother of Ravana defected and joined Rama's side, advising the vanara army?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother, defected to Rama and gave the army crucial counsel about Lanka's defenses.

questions.q: How many days did it take the vanara engineers to complete the great bridge to Lanka, according to the Valmiki Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana states that the vast bridge across the ocean was completed by the vanaras in five days.

questions.q: To whom did Angada go as a bold envoy before the battle, striding into the enemy court to deliver Rama's ultimatum?
questions.explanation: Angada went as Rama's envoy into Ravana's court, defiantly challenging him and demanding Sita's return.

questions.q: Which two strong vanaras, often named together, were among the chief warriors of the host?
questions.explanation: Mainda and Dvivida were celebrated as a powerful pair of vanara warriors among the leaders of the host.

questions.q: Which giant rakshasa, awakened to fight, rampaged through and devoured many of the vanara host in the war?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna, Ravana's colossal brother, woke from his deep sleep and tore through the vanara army before being slain by Rama.

questions.q: Which vanara is named among the prominent commanders who led divisions of the great army alongside Nila and Nala?
questions.explanation: Sharabha is named among the chief vanara commanders who led divisions of the great army.

questions.q: Whose son was Nala, the boon that gave him the divine skill to build the bridge of floating stones?
options: Vishvakarma, the architect of the gods
questions.explanation: Nala was a son of Vishvakarma, the architect of the gods, which gave him the skill to construct the great bridge.

questions.q: Which ancestor is named in tradition as the progenitor from whom Vali and Sugriva, the Kishkindha line, descended?
questions.explanation: Riksharajas is named in tradition as the progenitor from whom Vali and Sugriva descended, founders of the Kishkindha line.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the strength of the vanara host is described using vast numerical units such as kotis and which larger term for an uncountable multitude?
questions.explanation: Valmiki enumerates the army in immense numerical units such as kotis and maha-oghas to convey its uncountable vastness.
